title: asarum arifolium var. ruthii
intro: Asarum arifolium var. ruthii, also known as the Western wild ginger or Ruth's wild ginger, is a type of flowering plant native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin irritations. The health benefits of Asarum arifolium var. ruthii include its ability to reduce nausea and vomiting, as well as its potential to have antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. Asarum arifolium var. ruthii can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or dried root, which can be used to make infusions for medicinal purposes.
